subject: Re: Tomogram deconvolution in Warp
body:
Hi Mingyu,

It is a Wiener-type CTF deconvolution as described in the original Warp
paper, Supplementary Figure 2:

[image: image.png]
https://www.nature.com/articles/s41592-019-0580-y/figures/8

The intuition is to boost the very low frequencies to enhance contrast,
while attenuating everything past the first zero crossing of the CTF to
remove noise.

Best wishes,

--
Ricardo Diogo Righetto


Em sex., 23 de jan. de 2026 às 06:04, Mingyu Jung <jmg0910@snu.ac.kr>
escreveu:

> Hi all,
>
> I have a quick question regarding tomogram deconvolution in Warp (Windows).
>
> I was wondering what algorithm is used for tomogram deconvolution in Warp.
> Specifically, is it a Wiener-type CTF deconvolution, or is it limited to
> phase flipping only?
>
> Thank you very much for your help.
>
> Best,
> Mingyu
>
